data.scene: User writes a new NFC tag from the dashboard (Write NFC Tag).
data.purpose: Generate a unique tag identifier (UUID) and write it to a physical NFC tag for use with Home Assistant automations.
data.item: NFC Tag Identifier (UUID string written to physical NFC tag as URI)
data.frequency: On-demand (each time user taps 'Write NFC Tag')
data.processing: Local generation and NFC write; tag ID subsequently sent to Home Assistant server via REST API
data.storage: Not stored on device; tag ID is sent to Home Assistant server
data.recipient: Physical NFC tag (write target), Home Assistant server (tag scan event)
data.thirdparty: No third-party involvement; communication is between the device and the user's self-hosted Home Assistant server
data.switchpath: Dashboard -> NFC Tags -> Write NFC Tag

data.scene: User scans an NFC tag or QR code with the device.
data.purpose: Read the tag ID from a physical NFC tag or QR code and send it to the Home Assistant server to trigger automations.
data.source: Physical NFC tag (via NFC hardware) or QR code (via camera)
data.item: NFC Tag Identifier (extracted from tag or QR code content)
data.frequency: On-demand (each time user scans a tag)
data.processing: Local extraction; tag ID checked against local allowed-tags list (stored in app preferences); tag ID sent to Home Assistant server via REST API upon approval
data.storage: If user selects 'Allow Always', the tag ID is persisted in local app preferences for automatic future approval
data.recipient: Home Assistant server (tag scan event for automation triggering)
data.thirdparty: No third-party involvement; communication is between the device and the user's self-hosted Home Assistant server

permissions.name: NFC
permissions.purpose: Enable NFC hardware to read from and write to physical NFC tags
permissions.impact: If NFC is disabled, NFC tag reading and writing will be unavailable; QR code scanning is available as a fallback
